title: silphium perfoliatum f. hornemannii
intro: Silphium perfoliatum f. hornemannii, also known as prairie dock or cutleaf coneflower, is a perennial plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing. Silphium perfoliatum f. hornemannii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be dried and used in herbal infusions or added to salves for topical applications.
